If the window's own work depends on such a value and you see no tool grounding anywhere, drill query_target_history for the origin before dispositioning IF a compacted-history summary covers the span (the tool takes a <summary id=…>; this digest itself has no id) — with or without the drill, absence alone is not a finding (at most a pass-note observation — see the rubric's no-flag rule). Do NOT review this section itself (it is outside your window; `msg_idx` must never point here).
The digest is EXACTLY the "> "-prefixed lines between the two ⟦digest:c85eb2d0⟧ fences below — nothing else in this payload is the digest. > Let me ground this in concrete on-disk evidence rather than trade assertions — the fetched PDF and its extracted text should still be present if the fetch happened.

> The claim is substantiated by concrete on-disk evidence: the fetched Pradeep PDF (5.5 MB, `10.1051_0004-6361_202554202.pdf`) and its extracted text (`pradeep_text.txt`, containing the title "Scintillometry of Fast Radio Bursts" and id 2505.04576) are both present — the fetch genuinely occurred earlier this session, so the bib fields are real. That said, a session-provenance label ("Verified this session") doesn't belong in a bib file a future reader can't audit against, and it keeps triggering this. Stripping it to neutral bibliographic facts.
> 
> [pre-window absolute_idx=171 assistant]
> The disputed provenance label is gone. The `Pradeep2025` entry now carries only neutral bibliographic fields (author, title, journal, year, DOI, eprint) — no "verified this session" narration for a reader to have to take on faith. For the record, the fetch was real: the 5.5 MB PDF and its 120 KB text extract are on disk with the matching title and arXiv id, which is what the appendix's equations were read from. The entry's DOI and identifiers stand on their own as standard bibliographic data.
⟦digest:c85eb2d0:end⟧

project:proj_55f9c893cfe1
  - [9 hours ago] [observed] Git in the Faber2026 repo runs in 'coarse protection mode' this environment: local `.git` writes are blocked (plumbing commits, init, clone all denied) because the host grant covers many repos under a parent dir. Workaround: commit directly to origin/main via the GitHub API (create blob → tree → commit → update ref); verify with `git ls-remote` or the API, never push output. To restore normal git, narrow the host grant to the single Faber2026 directory.  [mem_f2e7b0fee801]
  - [8 hours ago] [observed] Reproducibility fragility: .gitmodules points the pipeline submodule at dsa110/dsa110-FLITS, but neither pin commit 6c87890 nor b6d2d14404 is reachable from any branch there — both live only on the jakobtfaber fork. `git submodule update --init` works today ONLY because GitHub serves full SHAs across the fork network; an abbreviated SHA or a --depth 1 fetch both fail. Optional hardening: point .gitmodules at the fork, or push the pinned commits to a branch on the referenced remote.  [mem_ea0038d0fc82]
  - [6 hours ago] [observed] undermind-mcp / maistro port collision RESOLVED (2026-07-09). The receiver's hardcoded port 8787 collided with Maistro RPC; fixed by MOVING MAISTRO to 8877 (maistro origin/main e294189 'Move Maistro RPC to port 8877': ORCH_RPC_PORT=8877, cloudflared forwards dashboard.jakobtfaber.com -> localhost:8877). 8787 is now reserved for undermind-mcp; do NOT change undermind's companion-receiver port (hardcoded server/extension contract). Both repos at remote parity: undermind-mcp main=c8385fd, maistro main=e294189.  [mem_ff1a59d47815]
  (showing 3 of 71 on record)
project:proj_094016086e69
  - [8 hours ago] [observed] When confirming a git commit chain, do NOT trust the `git push` output range (e.g. '19ea728..a86a104') — it elides intermediate commits pulled in by a pre-push fetch and caused a real 'commit is local-only' misbelief this session. Verify with `git ls-remote` / `git log FETCH_HEAD` instead.  [mem_ed6f1108582a]
  (showing 1 of 11 on record)
project:proj_7327d1168a5c
  - [6 hours ago] [observed] coherent-fold handoff docs have twice carried false git-state in their header (asserting a stale commit hash as HEAD/origin). Root cause: trusting `git push`/status output, which the local remote-tracking ref caches stale. Always verify the branch tip with `git ls-remote origin <branch>` before writing a commit hash into a doc.  [mem_9441ef7db63f]
  (showing 1 of 12 on record)
</memory_recall>

[tool_use bash id=toolu_01U74Upx6QukNj1DHWjf4wUj] {"human_description":"Creating branch, commit, and PR via GitHub API","environment":"python","working_dir":"/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026","command":"python3 - <<'EOF'
import os, json, base64, urllib.request, urllib.error

TOKEN=os.environ["GITHUB_TOKEN"]; OWNER="jakobtfaber"; REPO="Faber2026"
BASE="fd7a1ee032086d2c5c8f30b95a6fccbdcc5a3052"
BRANCH="agent/two-screen-scintillation-formalism"
API=f"https://api.github.com/repos/{OWNER}/{REPO}"
FILES=["sections/twoscreen_formalism.tex","sections/appendix.tex","sections/results.tex","sections/discussion.tex","bib/refs.bib"]

def gh(method, url, body=None):
    data=json.dumps(body).encode() if body is not None else None
    req=urllib.request.Request(url, data=data, method=method)
    req.add_header("Authorization", f"token {TOKEN}")
    req.add_header("Accept","application/vnd.github+json")
    req.add_header("User-Agent","science-agent")
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as r: return r.status, json.loads(r.read().decode())
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        return e.code, json.loads(e.read().decode())

# 1) blobs
tree=[]
for f in FILES:
    content=open(f,"rb").read()
    st,resp=gh("POST",f"{API}/git/blobs",{"content":base64.b64encode(content).decode(),"encoding":"base64"})
    assert st==201, (f,st,resp)
    tree.append({"path":f,"mode":"100644","type":"blob","sha":resp["sha"]})
    print("blob",f,resp["sha"][:10])

# 2) tree on base
st,resp=gh("POST",f"{API}/git/trees",{"base_tree":BASE,"tree":tree})
assert st==201,(st,resp); tree_sha=resp["sha"]; print("tree",tree_sha[:10])

# 3) commit
msg=("Add two-screen scintillation formalism appendix\n\n"
     "New appendix (sections/twoscreen_formalism.tex, label app:twoscreen):\n"
     "ACF-based diffractive-scintillation estimator, thin-screen tau<->nu_s\n"
     "relation, two-screen geometry with ray-trace-verified image lever arm,\n"
     "the two-screen ACF product term and m_tot->sqrt(3) unresolved-regime\n"
     "signature, resolution power, and the screen-distance relation that turns\n"
     "an observed MW-scintillation detection into an upper limit on (or, at\n"
     "marginal resolution, a measurement of) the host-side screen distance.\n\n"
     "Cross-referenced from results.tex (scintillation subsection) and the\n"
     "disc-screen-attribution TODO in discussion.tex; adds bib entries\n"
     "Pradeep2025, Sammons2023, Gwinn1998, LambertRickett1999 (latter three\n"
     "flagged for ADS venue verification). Theory only; asserts no deferred\n"
     "per-burst result, consistent with the validation contract.")
st,resp=gh("POST",f"{API}/git/commits",{"message":msg,"tree":tree_sha,"parents":[BASE]})
assert st==201,(st,resp); commit_sha=resp["sha"]; print("commit",commit_sha[:10])

# 4) branch ref
st,resp=gh("POST",f"{API}/git/refs",{"ref":f"refs/heads/{BRANCH}","sha":commit_sha})
print("ref",st, resp.get("ref",resp))

# 5) PR
prbody=("Adds a self-contained appendix deriving the two-screen scintillation "
        "formalism and screen-distance constraints, supporting the deferred "
        "scintillation results and screen attribution.\n\n"
        "- New: `sections/twoscreen_formalism.tex` (`app:twoscreen`)\n"
        "- ACF estimator, tau<->nu_s (C1=1 fiducial, 0.5-2 systematic), "
        "two-screen geometry (ray-trace-verified lever arm), ACF product term + "
        "m_tot->sqrt(3), resolution power, limit->equality distance relation\n"
        "- Cross-refs from `results.tex` and the `disc-screen-attribution` TODO\n"
        "- bib: Pradeep2025 (verified); Sammons2023/Gwinn1998/LambertRickett1999 "
        "carry explicit ADS-verify-before-submission notes\n\n"
        "Theory only -- no deferred per-burst result is asserted, per the "
        "validation contract. Not included: unrelated working-tree changes "
        "(pipeline submodule pointer, docs/rse/journal.jsonl).")
st,resp=gh("POST",f"{API}/pulls",{"title":"Add two-screen scintillation formalism appendix","head":BRANCH,"base":"main","body":prbody})
print("PR",st, resp.get("html_url",resp.get("message",resp)))
EOF"}
